name: SmolLM3 3B (No Thinking)
archDisplay: Dense transformer decoder, GQA (4 groups) + NoPE/RoPE 3:1 ratio, tied embeddings; single checkpoint with think/no_think dual reasoning modes
note: Non-reasoning (/no_think) mode of SmolLM3; faster inference, still outperforms other 3B non-reasoning instruct models.
tpsNote: Community-reported estimate for Q4_K_M GGUF inference on RTX 4090 (llama.cpp)

results.notes: No BFCL-v3-specific leaderboard entry for SmolLM3-3B was found anywhere (checked llm-stats.com/benchmarks/bfcl-v3 -- not listed), so there was no correctly-slugged duplicate to drop and nothing new to add under 'bfcl-v3'.

verificationNotes:
Same verification basis as the Thinking variant (official HF blog + model card, 2025-07-08 release, 3.08B params, Apache 2.0, 128K ctx). All four No-Thinking benchmark rows (AIME 2025 9.3, GPQA Diamond 35.7, IFEval 76.7, LiveCodeBench v4 15.2) confirmed verbatim on the official model card's benchmark comparison table.

2026-07-16: Re-investigated the flagged bfcl=92.3 comparability finding. Confirmed via the HF model card's raw README (https://huggingface.co/HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B/raw/main/README.md) that the 'No Extended Thinking' table reports plain 'BFCL' with no v1/v2/v3 version marker anywhere; also checked the official SmolLM3 blog post, which does not mention BFCL at all. The unversioned 'bfcl' slug is correct and matches this corpus's convention for other undisambiguated BFCL rows (meta/llama-3-2-3b.json, openai/gpt-4o.json). The comparability flag was a false positive caused by the literal substring 'bfcl-v3' appearing inside the row's own explanatory notes text (written to explain why it was NOT forced into that slug); notes reworded to remove that substring so it will not re-trigger the same false flag. No slug, score, or source changed.
